serving.natural_language_portion: One restaurant portion of Beijing Style Trio of Delicacies Tofu is approximately 350 g, comprising a generous block of silken or firm tofu braised with squid, shrimp, and shiitake mushrooms in a savoury broth, typically served as a shared main dish.

nutrient_source.title: Tofu with seafood composite dish — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Firm tofu (FDC ID 172475), Shrimp cooked (FDC ID 175180), Squid cooked (FDC ID 175168), Shiitake mushrooms cooked (FDC ID 169242), combined with standard Chinese braised tofu dish proportions
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Beijing-style trio tofu with squid, shrimp, and shiitake mushrooms exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values estimated by weighted-average composition of primary ingredients: firm tofu (~45% by weight), shrimp (~15%), squid (~15%), shiitake mushrooms (~10%), broth and seasonings (~15%). Proportions based on standard Chinese braised tofu dish formulations. Values cross-referenced against USDA SR Legacy entries for each component.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of tofu and soy-based foods — analogous estimate based on tofu GI values reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Beijing-style trio tofu with squid, shrimp, and shiitake mushrooms. GI estimated at 32 based on tofu GI values (GI ~15–35, mean ~32) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008). The dish is predominantly tofu with low-GI seafood and mushroom components; the broth and minimal cornstarch thickening contribute negligibly to glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (32 × 14.7) / 100 ≈ 4.7, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 4.2 × 3.5 = 14.7 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from broth and soy sauce seasoning
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 380 mg sodium per 100 g (1330 mg per 350 g serving), this dish contributes substantially to the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals; portion control and requesting reduced-sodium preparation are advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and high protein content supporting glycaemic control
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a low GI of approximately 32 and a glycaemic load of ~4.7 per serving, combined with 8.2 g protein per 100 g from tofu and seafood, this dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed as part of a balanced meal with attention to overall carbohydrate intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium load and cholesterol from seafood components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish provides beneficial soy protein and omega-3 fatty acids from seafood, which support cardiovascular health; however, the elevated sodium content (~380 mg/100 g) and moderate cholesterol (~52 mg/100 g) from shrimp and squid warrant portion moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beijing Style Trio of Delicacies Tofu (京城风味三鲜豆腐, Jīng chéng fēngwèi sān xiān dòufu) is a classic Northern Chinese braised tofu dish featuring three prized ingredients — squid, shrimp, and shiitake mushrooms — simmered together with silken or firm tofu in a rich, savoury broth. The dish originates from Beijing culinary tradition, where the concept of 'san xian' (三鲜, three delicacies) refers to a harmonious combination of premium ingredients that together create a layered umami flavour profile. The tofu is typically cut into large cubes and gently braised until it absorbs the broth, resulting in a silky, tender texture that contrasts with the springy bite of squid and shrimp and the earthy chewiness of shiitake mushrooms. The broth is seasoned with soy sauce, ginger, garlic, and sesame oil, and lightly thickened with cornstarch to create a glossy, clinging sauce. Nutritionally, this dish is a moderate-calorie, high-protein option providing approximately 78 kcal and 8.2 g protein per 100 g, with calcium from tofu, vitamin B12 from seafood, and dietary fibre from mushrooms. The low glycaemic index (~32) makes it suitable for blood sugar management. The primary clinical consideration is sodium content (~380 mg/100 g) from soy sauce and broth, which is relevant for hypertensive and cardiovascular patients. This dish is dairy-free and suitable for pescatarian diets. It is commonly served as a shared main course in Chinese restaurants in Singapore and across Southeast Asia.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific Beijing-style trio tofu dish was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values were estimated by weighted-average composition of primary ingredients (firm tofu, shrimp, squid, shiitake mushrooms, broth) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data. GI was estimated from analogous tofu GI values in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach rather than a direct dish measurement.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for firm tofu (FDC 172475), cooked shrimp (FDC 175180), cooked squid (FDC 175168), and cooked shiitake mushrooms (FDC 169242), weighted by approximate dish proportions. Direct measurement of this specific dish is recommended for higher confidence. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as vitamin D is not reliably reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries used for this estimation; the individual component records for firm tofu (FDC 172475), cooked shrimp (FDC 175180), cooked squid (FDC 175168), and cooked shiitake mushrooms (FDC 169242) do not provide a defensible vitamin D value, and no approved primary source reports vitamin D for this composite dish. 3. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g as this is a non-fried, broth-based dish using no hydrogenated fats; confirmed negligible per USDA component data. 4. GI of 32 is an analogous estimate based on tofu GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care; no direct GI measurement for this composite dish exists in approved sources. 5. Halal status is listed as false because the dish contains shellfish (shrimp) and molluscs (squid), which are not universally accepted as halal under all Islamic jurisprudence schools, and the restaurant source does not carry a verified halal certification. 6. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are estimated from USDA component data; trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed negligible by formulation). 7. The food is served at See Ya Tonight 今晚有局 - Singapore Level 1 via field survey data at SGD 24.44.
